lead:
**Cloud-Skimmers** are sleek, fast airships designed for high-altitude travel across [[Aethelgard]]. Engineered by the brilliant aeronaut **Vessa Cloudbrace** in 1812 AE, they operate at altitudes where conventional airships lose buoyancy, sustained by a hybrid system of gas-filled envelopes and [[Aether-Crystals]]-powered turbines.

The [[Sky-Merchants Guild]] adopted Cloud-Skimmers as their premier courier vessels almost immediately after their first public demonstration, and today operates the largest fleet of any organisation. At cruising altitudes of 3,800 to 8,000 metres, Cloud-Skimmers fly above most weather systems and reach destinations faster than any ground-based transport — the journey from Aethelheim to Port Azure, which takes six days overland, takes under 18 hours by Cloud-Skimmer.

content.content:
A Cloud-Skimmer's most distinctive feature is its **dual-lift system**: a conventional aerostatic envelope filled with *skygas* (a refined aetheric gas lighter than air) provides passive buoyancy, while two lateral **aetheric turbines** — powered by Grade-B or Grade-C [[Aether-Crystals]] — provide active thrust and altitude control.

The hull is constructed from **aether-tempered aluminium**, an alloy developed specifically for Cloud-Skimmer production that maintains structural integrity at extreme temperatures and pressures. The cockpit is enclosed and pressurised — a requirement at altitudes above 6,000 metres where unprotected crew would face oxygen deprivation.

content.caption: Technical drawing of a Mark III Cloud-Skimmer, from the Guild Navigation Manual
Archival footage: First public demonstration of the Cloud-Skimmer prototype, Port Azure, 1812 AE (colourised and restored)

content.content: Cloud-Skimmers have gone through five major design iterations since their introduction in 1812 AE, each **Mark** representing a substantial engineering advancement. The current production model is the **Mark V**, first flown in 1918 AE and featuring the most efficient turbine design yet achieved.

content.title: Altitude Record
content.content: The Cloud-Skimmer altitude record of 12,400 metres was set in 1922 AE by Pilot Orion Starcross during a Guild-sponsored research flight. At this altitude, the vessel was above 98% of Aethelgard's atmospheric weather systems.

One of the most technically demanding Cloud-Skimmer applications is the **Rift Transit** — passage over or near the [[Great Aetheric Rift]]. The raw aetheric energy emanating from the rift interferes severely with standard [[Aether-Crystals]] crystal navigation and can overwhelm a standard turbine core.

Mark IV and V Cloud-Skimmers can be fitted with a **Rift Shield Package**: a series of copper-wound crystal dampeners that regulate the ambient energy input to the turbines. Only 47 Guild vessels are currently certified for Rift Transit, and the certification requires annual renewal after inspection.

items.caption: Guild Cloud-Skimmer fleet at Port Azure, spring mooring
A Mark V above the cloud layer at 7,200 metres
A Rift-shielded Mark IV approaching the Great Aetheric Rift

content.content:
Cloud-Skimmer pilots are among the most highly trained and well-compensated professionals in [[Aethelgard]]. A full certification requires a minimum of three years of study at a Guild-approved aviation academy, 500 hours of supervised flight time on conventional airships, and 100 hours of Cloud-Skimmer co-pilot time before solo certification.

There are currently approximately 2,400 certified Cloud-Skimmer pilots in Aethelgard, of whom roughly 1,800 are active Guild employees. The remainder fly for private clients — a practice the Guild permits under a licensing regime that keeps routes and cargo manifests under Guild oversight.
Up there, there's no Dominion, no Collective, no politics. There's only you, the crystal, and the sky.
content.author: Senior Pilot Ilara Windchaser, Guild Navigation Quarterly, 1919 AE
